description: A Sustainability Specialist for a commercial real estate firm is evaluating a 500 kW roof-mounted PV system in Phoenix, AZ to determine if a West-facing orientation yields more financial value than a South-facing one under a specific Time-of-Use (TOU) rate. Using PySAM and the weather data at /home/ga/SAM_Weather_Data/phoenix_az_tmy.csv, simulate a 500 kW commercial PV system (DC/AC Ratio: 1.2, Array Type: 1, Tilt: 20 degrees, System Losses: 14%, Inverter Efficiency: 96%) for two scenarios: South-facing (Azimuth 180°) and West-facing (Azimuth 270°). Calculate the annual financial value for both orientations by applying an hourly TOU pricing array to the generated power. The Peak Rate ($0.35/kWh) applies during June, July, August, and September (Months 6-9) during hours 16, 17, and 18 (4:00 PM to 6:59 PM). The Off-Peak Rate ($0.08/kWh) applies to all other hours of the 8760-hour year. Assume a standard non-leap year of 8760 hours starting on January 1st at hour 0. Save the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/tou_orientation_value.json using the following schema: { "south_annual_energy_kwh": <float>, "west_annual_energy_kwh": <float>, "south_annual_value_usd": <float>, "west_annual_value_usd": <float>, "higher_value_orientation": "<South or West>" }.
